the vyaktis in question just *are* the dhvanis

denials.name: generative_cause
denials.cite: so that the sound-word relation becomes a special case of the particular-universal relation

claims.cite: individuals are construed as the sounds

rejected_readings.label: sphota_as_particular
rejected_readings.rendering: The sphota is an individual sound instance, not a universal type.
rejected_readings.why: Confuses the sphota (universal jati) with vyaktis (individual instances). Demoting sphota to a particular entity breaks the whole universal-particular framework and makes the identity with jati false.

rejected_readings.label: dhvani_as_generative_cause
rejected_readings.rendering: Sounds (dhvani) are the generative/productive cause of the sphota, rather than its manifestations.
rejected_readings.why: Misreads the manifestation relation as a causal generation. The commentary establishes that 'the sound-word relation becomes a special case of the particular-universal relation'—sounds must be instances of the universal (type), not independent causes. Treating dhvani as a generative cause of sphota contradicts this denial and reintroduces the two-relation problem: one causal relation (dhvani→sphota) plus the type-instance relation. The economy argument requires only one unified relation.

notes: Verse 1.93 reports the jati-sphota systematization as received doctrine (smrta) without necessarily endorsing it as Bhartrhari's own position; the contract encodes the view's internal coherence: sphota = universal, manifested by individual instances that are identical with sounds.
